description: A hands-on end-to-end capstone that takes a simple Flask web app all the way through Docker containerization and deployment on a local Kubernetes cluster (minikube/kind). Starting from a single line of code, you will work through a Dockerfile, cluster setup, Deployment/Service/ConfigMap manifests, rolling updates, and rollback — covering the full pipeline of app → image → cluster → deployment → operations in one continuous journey. Designed for learners who have encountered Pod, Deployment, and Service concepts in the K8S series (k8s-01–35) and want to see how those pieces fit together by building and shipping a real application themselves.
summary: Build a Flask app, containerize it with Docker, and deploy it to a local Kubernetes cluster in this end-to-end capstone.
target_audience: Docker/Kubernetes beginners who have been introduced to Pod/Deployment/Service concepts in the existing K8S series and are ready to wire everything together in a capstone project. Basic Python knowledge and comfort with a terminal are sufficient.

lectures.title: Building a Simple Flask Web App
objectives: Build a minimal web and REST application in Flask with a couple of endpoints (/ and /api) and run it locally.
Add a dedicated health-check route (/healthz) that returns HTTP 200, so the app is ready for Kubernetes probes later.
Declare dependencies in a requirements.txt file and start the app with either flask run or python app.py to confirm it works.

lectures.title: Containerizing the App with Docker
objectives: Write a Dockerfile that selects a base image, installs dependencies, and sets a CMD to package the Flask app as an image.
Build the image with docker build and run it as a container with docker run, verifying port exposure (EXPOSE / -p).
Tag the image with a meaningful name and version (name:version) using docker tag in preparation for Kubernetes deployment.

lectures.title: Setting Up a Local Kubernetes Cluster
objectives: Install minikube or kind and start a local Kubernetes cluster.
Use kubectl to verify cluster connectivity and node status (kubectl get nodes / cluster-info).
Load a locally built image into the cluster via minikube image load or kind load docker-image to enable deployment without a registry.
keywords: minikube image load kind load docker-image

lectures.title: Writing Kubernetes Manifests
objectives: Write Deployment and Service (plus ConfigMap if needed) YAML manifests for the Flask app.
Align labels and selectors (matchLabels) and correctly specify replicas, container image, and containerPort.
Configure lightweight readiness and liveness probes against /healthz and learn to separate configuration using a ConfigMap.

lectures.title: Deploy, Expose, Verify, and Rolling Update
objectives: Deploy manifests with kubectl apply and validate Pod and Service state using get/describe/logs.
Access the app externally via kubectl port-forward or NodePort/Ingress and confirm it responds correctly.
Perform a zero-downtime rolling update with kubectl set image / rollout and roll back with rollout undo.
keywords: kubernetes rolling update set image rollout
